items.label: storefront-dev

shellCommands.command: # Check what shared/ui exports for Modal components ↵ grep -n 'Modal' /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/node_modules/@salesforce/retail-react-app/app/components/shared/ui/index.js 2>/dev/n

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ # Wait for server to be ready ↵ for i in $(seq 1 90); do ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git diff HEAD --name-only 2>&1 && echo "---STAGED---" && git diff --cached --name-only 2>&1 && echo "---UNTRACKED---" && git ls-files --others --exclude-standard apps/commer

messages: Now I have a thorough understanding of the spec. Let me explore the existing codebase and reference files.
There are already files from a previous attempt. Let me check if there's an existing state or pending context, then examine these files and run tests.
Good, there's already a solid implementation. Now let me check the test files and run the tests to see their current status.
Good, the implementation and tests look complete. Let me run the tests to see their current status.
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server and SSR for the affected routes.

All three routes return HTTP 200: - `/` → 200 ✅ - `/category/newarrivals` → 200 ✅ (PLP with product tiles) - `/search?q=shirt` → 200 ✅ (search results with product tiles)  The SSR is working perfectly with our overrides. Now let me check if there's a validation hook file to update, and check for any uncommitted changes.
The validation hook already has PLP and search route checks. The implementation looks complete. Let me verify the git status shows no dirty working tree for our files (they may already be committed).
The files exist but haven't been committed yet (they're not in the git log). Let me check the exact git status of our implementation files.

The implementation is already committed on the current `feature/product-quick-view` branch (commit `d012578`). Let me do a final summary of what's verified:  **Implementation Summary:** - ✅ `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` — ProductTile override with full-width Quick View overlay bar (slide-up on desktop hover, always visible on mobile) - ✅ `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` — QuickViewModal using `useProductViewModal` hook + `ProductView` with error/loading states - ✅ `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` — 12 unit tests (overlay bar rendering, interaction, visual states) - ✅ `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` — 14 unit tests (modal shell, content, accessibility) - ✅ **26/26 unit tests passing** - ✅ **SSR validation**: `/` → 200, `/category/newarrivals` → 200, `/search?q=shirt` → 200 - ✅ Validation hook already covers PLP and search routes - ✅ All files committed (commit `d012578`)

reportedOutcome.docNote: Product Quick View implemented via PWA Kit override pattern: ProductTile override wraps base tile with slide-up overlay bar (ViewIcon + "Quick View" text, _groupHover animation), QuickViewModal reuses useProductViewModal hook + ProductView with loading spinner, error state, and aria-label. Sets/bundles excluded. 26 unit tests pass, SSR verified on PLP and search routes.
reportedOutcome.handoffArtifact: {"testIds":{"quickViewButton":"quick-view-btn","quickViewModal":"quick-view-modal","quickViewSpinner":"quick-view-spinner","quickViewError":"quick-view-error"},"routes":["/category/newarrivals","/search?q=shirt"],"triggerBehavior":"Click quick-view-btn on any product tile to open modal. Bar slides up on desktop hover, always visible on mobile.","modalContent":"ProductView with imageSize=sm, showFullLink=true, variant selectors, Add to Cart button.","excludedProductTypes":["set","bundle"]}
